passage.title: Morning Schedule Board
passage.text: Our group keeps a job wheel near the reading shelf. In the morning, Yuna points to it before reading time. The job wheel has picture cards for reading, math, lunch, and playtime for the day's job. Ms. Park asks us to follow the chart and start class calmly. Sometimes we compare today's card with yesterday's card and notice one small change. We like the job wheel because it helps us know our order of jobs.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the job wheel is used.
The first sentence says the job wheel is near the reading shelf.

questions.prompt: When does Yuna use the job wheel?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Yuna uses it in the morning.

questions.prompt: What does the job wheel have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the job wheel has picture cards.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
